subject: [RFC PATCH 39/40] mm: compaction: respect tainted SPB reserve in destination selection

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's compaction algorithm to respect tainted superpageblocks (SPBs) when selecting destination pages for migration. The goal is to prevent compaction from eroding the reserve headroom in tainted SPBs, which is necessary for satisfying non-movable allocations without tainting fresh clean SPBs. The change introduces a check that refuses tainted-SPB pageblocks as compaction destinations once the number of free pages falls below the reserved amount. This bias towards producing free pageblocks inside clean SPBs can help with hugepage allocation and, combined with other changes, enables kcompactd to handle clean-SPB consolidation alone.
